title: PROP-FEED-S5-005 (TS/Rust parity) is the defining cross-language contract for NoteId format yet is marked required=false and only one manual snapshot pair
description:
PROP-FEED-S5-005 is described as: 'TS/Rust NoteId フォーマット parity — Rust next_available_note_id と TS nextAvailableNoteId が同一 (now_ms, existing) 入力に対して同一出力を返す. 手動スナップショット比較 1 件 (固定 clock + 固定 existing)'. The verification tier table at line 737 marks it `Required: false`. This is the only obligation that actually verifies that the Rust port is faithful to the canonical TS algorithm — every other PROP in Sprint 5 (S5-001..S5-004) tests the Rust side in isolation. If S5-005 is skipped, divergence between TS and Rust will not be caught: e.g., a Rust impl that uses `chrono::Utc::now()` but returns a slightly different padding rule, or one that uses `format!("{:04}-...", year)` (which produces '-001' for year 1 instead of TS's '1' produced by `String(1).padStart(2,'0')` which actually returns '01' — and getUTCFullYear() never pads). Sprint 4 PROP-FEED-S4-016 made the same parity test required=true with the same '1 snapshot pair' scope. Sprint 5's S5-005 inexplicably downgrades it to required=false despite being the analogous parity obligation for a different algorithm. The 'rationale' field is absent.

Additionally, 'manual snapshot comparison 1 ペア' is insufficient as evidence of parity even when required=true: a single fixed input does not cover (a) collision suffix paths (`-1`, `-10`), (b) UTC boundary conditions (year transitions, leap seconds), (c) timezone interpretation (if a Rust impl accidentally uses local time, a fixed UTC noon input may still match by coincidence). Sprint 4's S4-016 explicitly deferred fast-check parity to Sprint 5; Sprint 5 in turn defers it without naming a target sprint.
evidence:
verification-architecture.md:710 — 'PROP-FEED-S5-005 | ... | 1 | 手動スナップショット比較 (cargo test + vitest) | false | pure (cross-language)'
verification-architecture.md:737 — Sprint 5 Verification Tier Table marks S5-005 'Required: false'
verification-architecture.md:769 (Sprint 4 deferral note) — 'fast-check による全 markdown 任意入力での parity 検証は Sprint 5 へ deferral する'
promptnotes/src/lib/domain/app-startup/initialize-capture.ts:112-126 — formatBaseId uses padStart(2)/padStart(3); collision suffix loop at lines 98-103
remediation: Mark PROP-FEED-S5-005 as `Required: true` and expand the test matrix to at least: (a) base case (no collision, single-digit and double-digit time fields), (b) collision -1 suffix, (c) collision -10 suffix, (d) UTC edge: now_ms = 0 (Unix epoch) and now_ms near 32-bit overflow. Each as a distinct snapshot pair (cargo test fixture + matching vitest fixture sharing the same input JSON). If exhaustive fast-check parity is to be deferred, name the target sprint and the precondition for closing the deferral. As written, the spec allows the Rust port to silently diverge from the canonical TS algorithm and still pass the Phase 5 gate.
